sourceMovie.title: Just Friends
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Just Friends for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Roger Kumble keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Groundhog Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Just Friends clicked for you, Groundhog Day (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Just Friends?
faq.answer: Try Groundhog Day, The Holiday, The Apartment—each shares DNA with Just Friends while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Just Friends?
faq.answer: Just Friends (2005) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Just Friends worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
